---
title: "Colorado Retail Sales"
format:
  dashboard:
    theme: cosmo
    nav-buttons:
      - icon: github
        href: https://cdor.colorado.gov/retail-sales-reports
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{r setup}
library(tidyverse)
library(arrow)
library(scales)
library(sdotools)

# Load pre-built parquet files (run R/collect_data.R to refresh)
state_df          <- read_parquet("data/state.parquet")
county_df         <- read_parquet("data/county.parquet")
city_df           <- read_parquet("data/city.parquet")
county_ind_df     <- read_parquet("data/county_industry.parquet")
city_ind_df       <- read_parquet("data/city_industry.parquet")
state_ind_df      <- read_parquet("data/state_industry.parquet")

# Helpers -----------------------------------------------------------------

yoy_growth <- function(df, value_col = retail_sales, group_cols = c()) {
  df |>
    arrange(across(all_of(c(group_cols, "year", "month")))) |>
    group_by(across(all_of(group_cols))) |>
    mutate(
      yoy = (.data[[deparse(substitute(value_col))]] /
               lag(.data[[deparse(substitute(value_col))]], 12) - 1) * 100
    ) |>
    ungroup()
}

# Compute YoY for state
state_yoy <- state_df |>
  arrange(year, month) |>
  mutate(yoy = (retail_sales / lag(retail_sales, 12) - 1) * 100)

# Latest month for headlines
latest <- state_df |> slice_max(date, n = 1)
prev_yr <- state_df |>
  filter(year == latest$year - 1L, month == latest$month) |>
  slice(1)

# Counties list (excluding "Total" aggregate)
counties <- county_df |>
  filter(county != "Total") |>
  distinct(county) |>
  pull(county) |>
  sort()

cities <- city_df |>
  distinct(city) |>
  pull(city) |>
  sort()

industries <- state_ind_df |>
  filter(industry != "Total") |>
  distinct(industry) |>
  pull(industry) |>
  sort()

# Pass key data to OJS
ojs_define(
  state_data      = state_yoy,
  county_data     = county_df |>
    filter(county != "Total") |>
    arrange(county, year, month) |>
    group_by(county) |>
    mutate(yoy = (retail_sales / lag(retail_sales, 12) - 1) * 100) |>
    ungroup(),
  city_data       = city_df |>
    arrange(city, year, month) |>
    group_by(city) |>
    mutate(yoy = (retail_sales / lag(retail_sales, 12) - 1) * 100) |>
    ungroup(),
  county_ind_data = county_ind_df,
  city_ind_data   = city_ind_df,
  state_ind_data  = state_ind_df,
  counties_list   = counties,
  cities_list     = cities,
  industries_list = industries,
  latest_month    = format(latest$date, "%B %Y"),
  latest_sales    = latest$retail_sales,
  latest_yoy      = round(state_yoy |> slice_max(date, n = 1) |> pull(yoy), 1)
)
```

# State Overview

## Row {height="15%"}

```{r}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Statewide Retail Sales"
list(
  icon  = "shop",
  color = "primary",
  value = dollar(latest$retail_sales, scale = 1e-9, suffix = "B", accuracy = 0.1)
)
```

```{r}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Year-over-Year Change"
yoy_val <- state_yoy |> slice_max(date, n = 1) |> pull(yoy)
list(
  icon  = if (yoy_val >= 0) "arrow-up" else "arrow-down",
  color = if (yoy_val >= 0) "success" else "danger",
  value = paste0(sprintf("%+.1f", yoy_val), "%")
)
```

```{r}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Latest Month"
list(
  icon  = "calendar3",
  color = "secondary",
  value = format(latest$date, "%B %Y")
)
```

## Row

```{ojs}
//| title: "Monthly Retail Sales (Statewide)"

import { Plot } from "@observablehq/plot"

viewof time_unit = Inputs.radio(["Monthly", "Annual"], { value: "Monthly", label: "View:" })

{
  const data = transpose(state_data)
  const field = time_unit === "Annual" ? "year" : "date"

  let plotData = data.filter(d => !isNaN(d.retail_sales))

  if (time_unit === "Annual") {
    // Roll up to annual
    const byYear = d3.rollup(plotData, v => ({
      year: v[0].year,
      retail_sales: d3.sum(v, d => d.retail_sales),
      gross_sales: d3.sum(v, d => d.gross_sales)
    }), d => d.year)
    plotData = [...byYear.values()]
  }

  return Plot.plot({
    width: 800,
    height: 320,
    x: { label: null },
    y: { label: "Retail Sales ($)", tickFormat: d => `$${(d/1e9).toFixed(1)}B` },
    marks: [
      Plot.areaY(plotData, {
        x: time_unit === "Annual" ? "year" : (d => new Date(d.date)),
        y: "retail_sales",
        fill: "#2E6DA4",
        fillOpacity: 0.2
      }),
      Plot.lineY(plotData, {
        x: time_unit === "Annual" ? "year" : (d => new Date(d.date)),
        y: "retail_sales",
        stroke: "#2E6DA4",
        strokeWidth: 2,
        tip: true
      })
    ]
  })
}
```

```{ojs}
//| title: "Year-over-Year Growth Rate"

{
  const data = transpose(state_data).filter(d => d.yoy != null && !isNaN(d.yoy))
  return Plot.plot({
    width: 800,
    height: 260,
    x: { label: null },
    y: { label: "YoY %", tickFormat: d => `${d.toFixed(1)}%` },
    marks: [
      Plot.ruleY([0], { stroke: "#999" }),
      Plot.lineY(data, {
        x: d => new Date(d.date),
        y: "yoy",
        stroke: d => d.yoy >= 0 ? "#2ca02c" : "#d62728",
        strokeWidth: 2,
        tip: true
      })
    ]
  })
}
```

# Geography

## Row {.sidebar}

```{ojs}
viewof geo_level = Inputs.radio(["County", "City"], { value: "County", label: "Geography:" })

viewof selected_geos = Inputs.select(
  geo_level === "County" ? counties_list : cities_list,
  {
    value: geo_level === "County"
      ? counties_list.slice(0, 5)
      : cities_list.filter(c => ["Denver", "Aurora", "Colorado Springs", "Fort Collins", "Boulder"].includes(c)),
    multiple: true,
    label: geo_level === "County" ? "Counties:" : "Cities:",
    size: 15
  }
)

viewof geo_metric = Inputs.radio(
  ["Retail Sales", "YoY Growth"],
  { value: "Retail Sales", label: "Metric:" }
)

viewof geo_time = Inputs.radio(["Monthly", "Annual"], { value: "Annual", label: "View:" })
```

## Row

```{ojs}
//| title: "Retail Sales by Geography vs. State Average"

{
  const raw = geo_level === "County"
    ? transpose(county_data)
    : transpose(city_data)

  const geoCol = geo_level === "County" ? "county" : "city"
  const selected = new Set(selected_geos)

  let data = raw.filter(d => selected.has(d[geoCol]))

  // Aggregate to annual if needed
  if (geo_time === "Annual") {
    const grouped = d3.rollup(data, v => ({
      [geoCol]: v[0][geoCol],
      year: v[0].year,
      retail_sales: d3.sum(v, d => d.retail_sales ?? 0)
    }), d => `${d[geoCol]}_${d.year}`)
    data = [...grouped.values()]
  }

  const metric = geo_metric === "YoY Growth" ? "yoy" : "retail_sales"
  const yFmt   = geo_metric === "YoY Growth"
    ? d => `${(+d).toFixed(1)}%`
    : d => `$${(+d / 1e6).toFixed(0)}M`
  const yLabel = geo_metric === "YoY Growth" ? "YoY %" : "Retail Sales ($M)"

  // State average line for comparison
  const stateAvg = geo_time === "Annual"
    ? d3.rollup(
        transpose(state_data),
        v => ({ year: v[0].year, retail_sales: d3.sum(v, d => d.retail_sales ?? 0) }),
        d => d.year
      )
    : null

  return Plot.plot({
    width: 760,
    height: 360,
    color: { legend: true },
    x: { label: null },
    y: { label: yLabel, tickFormat: yFmt },
    marks: [
      Plot.lineY(data, {
        x: geo_time === "Annual" ? "year" : (d => new Date(d.date)),
        y: metric,
        stroke: geoCol,
        tip: true,
        strokeWidth: 2
      }),
      ...(geo_time === "Annual" && metric === "retail_sales" ? [
        Plot.lineY([...stateAvg.values()], {
          x: "year",
          y: "retail_sales",
          stroke: "#999",
          strokeDasharray: "4,3",
          strokeWidth: 1.5
        })
      ] : [])
    ]
  })
}
```

```{ojs}
//| title: "Latest Month: Retail Sales Ranking"

{
  const raw = geo_level === "County"
    ? transpose(county_data)
    : transpose(city_data)
  const geoCol = geo_level === "County" ? "county" : "city"

  // Most recent date
  const maxDate = d3.max(raw, d => d.date)
  const latest  = raw.filter(d => d.date === maxDate && d.retail_sales != null)

  const stateTotal = transpose(state_data).find(d => d.date === maxDate)?.retail_sales ?? 1

  const sorted = latest
    .sort((a, b) => b.retail_sales - a.retail_sales)
    .slice(0, 20)
    .map(d => ({ ...d, share: d.retail_sales / stateTotal * 100 }))

  const selected = new Set(selected_geos)

  return Plot.plot({
    width: 760,
    height: 480,
    marginLeft: 130,
    x: { label: "Retail Sales", tickFormat: d => `$${(d/1e6).toFixed(0)}M` },
    y: { label: null },
    color: { scheme: "tableau10" },
    marks: [
      Plot.barX(sorted, {
        x: "retail_sales",
        y: geoCol,
        sort: { y: "-x" },
        fill: d => selected.has(d[geoCol]) ? "#2E6DA4" : "#ccc",
        tip: true
      }),
      Plot.ruleX([0])
    ]
  })
}
```

# Industry

## Row {.sidebar}

```{ojs}
viewof ind_geo_level = Inputs.radio(["State", "County", "City"], { value: "State", label: "Geography:" })

viewof ind_selected_geo = {
  if (ind_geo_level === "State") return Inputs.text({ value: "Colorado", disabled: true, label: "Area:" })
  const opts = ind_geo_level === "County" ? counties_list : cities_list
  return Inputs.select(opts, { value: opts[0], label: "Area:" })
}

viewof ind_time = Inputs.radio(["Monthly", "Annual"], { value: "Annual", label: "View:" })

viewof selected_industries = Inputs.select(
  industries_list,
  {
    value: industries_list.slice(0, 8),
    multiple: true,
    label: "Industries:",
    size: 12
  }
)
```

## Row

```{ojs}
//| title: "Retail Sales by Industry"

{
  let raw, geoCol

  if (ind_geo_level === "State") {
    raw = transpose(state_ind_data)
    geoCol = null
  } else if (ind_geo_level === "County") {
    raw = transpose(county_ind_data)
    geoCol = "county"
  } else {
    raw = transpose(city_ind_data)
    geoCol = "city"
  }

  let data = raw.filter(d =>
    (geoCol == null || d[geoCol] === ind_selected_geo) &&
    selected_industries.includes(d.industry) &&
    d.retail_sales != null
  )

  if (ind_time === "Annual") {
    const grouped = d3.rollup(data, v => ({
      year: v[0].year,
      industry: v[0].industry,
      retail_sales: d3.sum(v, d => d.retail_sales ?? 0)
    }), d => `${d.industry}_${d.year}`)
    data = [...grouped.values()]
  }

  return Plot.plot({
    width: 760,
    height: 380,
    color: { legend: true },
    x: { label: null },
    y: { label: "Retail Sales", tickFormat: d => `$${(d/1e6).toFixed(0)}M` },
    marks: [
      Plot.lineY(data, {
        x: ind_time === "Annual" ? "year" : (d => new Date(d.date)),
        y: "retail_sales",
        stroke: "industry",
        tip: true,
        strokeWidth: 1.8
      })
    ]
  })
}
```

```{ojs}
//| title: "Industry Mix — Latest Month"

{
  let raw, geoCol

  if (ind_geo_level === "State") {
    raw = transpose(state_ind_data)
    geoCol = null
  } else if (ind_geo_level === "County") {
    raw = transpose(county_ind_data)
    geoCol = "county"
  } else {
    raw = transpose(city_ind_data)
    geoCol = "city"
  }

  const maxDate = d3.max(raw, d => d.date)
  const latest = raw.filter(d =>
    d.date === maxDate &&
    (geoCol == null || d[geoCol] === ind_selected_geo) &&
    d.industry !== "Total" &&
    d.retail_sales != null
  )

  const sorted = [...latest].sort((a, b) => b.retail_sales - a.retail_sales)

  return Plot.plot({
    width: 760,
    height: 460,
    marginLeft: 210,
    x: { label: "Retail Sales", tickFormat: d => `$${(d/1e6).toFixed(0)}M` },
    y: { label: null },
    marks: [
      Plot.barX(sorted, {
        x: "retail_sales",
        y: "industry",
        sort: { y: "-x" },
        fill: "#2E6DA4",
        fillOpacity: 0.8,
        tip: true
      }),
      Plot.ruleX([0])
    ]
  })
}
```

# Outperformance

## Row

::: {.callout-note}
**Outperformance** = a county or city whose year-over-year retail sales growth exceeds the statewide rate in the same month. Higher positive values indicate stronger-than-average growth.
:::

```{ojs}
//| title: "Controls"

viewof out_geo_level = Inputs.radio(["County", "City"], { value: "County", label: "Geography:" })
viewof out_year = Inputs.range([2017, 2026], { value: 2025, step: 1, label: "Year:" })
viewof out_industry = Inputs.select(
  ["All Industries", ...industries_list],
  { value: "All Industries", label: "Industry:" }
)
```

## Row

```{ojs}
//| title: "Excess Growth vs. State Average"

{
  // Annual YoY by geography
  const raw = out_geo_level === "County"
    ? transpose(county_data)
    : transpose(city_data)
  const geoCol = out_geo_level === "County" ? "county" : "city"

  // State annual totals for reference year and prior
  const stateByYear = d3.rollup(
    transpose(state_data),
    v => d3.sum(v, d => d.retail_sales ?? 0),
    d => d.year
  )
  const stateGrowth = (stateByYear.get(out_year) / stateByYear.get(out_year - 1) - 1) * 100

  // Geo annual totals
  const geoByYearGeo = d3.rollup(
    raw.filter(d => d.retail_sales != null),
    v => d3.sum(v, d => d.retail_sales ?? 0),
    d => `${d[geoCol]}_${d.year}`
  )

  const geos = [...new Set(raw.map(d => d[geoCol]))]

  const results = geos.flatMap(geo => {
    const cur  = geoByYearGeo.get(`${geo}_${out_year}`)     ?? null
    const prev = geoByYearGeo.get(`${geo}_${out_year - 1}`) ?? null
    if (!cur || !prev || prev === 0) return []
    const geoGrowth = (cur / prev - 1) * 100
    return [{ geo, growth: geoGrowth, excess: geoGrowth - stateGrowth, sales: cur }]
  })

  const sorted = results.sort((a, b) => b.excess - a.excess).slice(0, 25)

  return Plot.plot({
    width: 780,
    height: 540,
    marginLeft: 140,
    x: {
      label: "Excess Growth vs State (%)",
      tickFormat: d => `${d > 0 ? "+" : ""}${d.toFixed(1)}%`
    },
    y: { label: null },
    color: {
      domain: [true, false],
      range: ["#2ca02c", "#d62728"],
      legend: true,
      tickFormat: d => d ? "Outperforming" : "Underperforming"
    },
    marks: [
      Plot.barX(sorted, {
        x: "excess",
        y: "geo",
        sort: { y: "-x" },
        fill: d => d.excess >= 0,
        tip: {
          format: {
            x: d => `${d > 0 ? "+" : ""}${d.toFixed(1)}% vs state`,
            fill: false
          }
        }
      }),
      Plot.ruleX([0], { stroke: "#333", strokeWidth: 2 }),
      Plot.text(sorted.filter(d => d.excess >= 0), {
        x: "excess",
        y: "geo",
        text: d => `+${d.excess.toFixed(1)}%`,
        dx: 4,
        fontSize: 11,
        fill: "#2ca02c",
        textAnchor: "start"
      })
    ]
  })
}
```

```{ojs}
//| title: "State YoY Benchmark"

{
  const stateByYear = d3.rollup(
    transpose(state_data),
    v => d3.sum(v, d => d.retail_sales ?? 0),
    d => d.year
  )
  const stateGrowth = (stateByYear.get(out_year) / stateByYear.get(out_year - 1) - 1) * 100

  return html`<div style="padding: 1rem; background: #f0f4ff; border-radius: 8px; font-size: 1.1rem;">
    <strong>State YoY (${out_year}):</strong>
    <span style="color: ${stateGrowth >= 0 ? '#2ca02c' : '#d62728'}; font-weight: bold;">
      ${stateGrowth >= 0 ? "+" : ""}${stateGrowth.toFixed(1)}%
    </span>
    <br/>Geographies above this line are outperforming the state average.
  </div>`
}
```
